title: Docker image build and publish to registry via CI
description: Extend the project's CI (TASK-8) with a workflow that builds the production Dockerfile (TASK-6) and pushes it to a public registry (Docker Hub or GHCR) on release, so third parties can pull a ready-made image.
acceptanceCriteria: `.github/workflows/docker-publish.yml` builds the image from the root `Dockerfile` and pushes it to a chosen registry (GHCR recommended — no extra account needed beyond GitHub)
Workflow triggers on version tags (e.g. `v*`) and/or pushes to the default branch, tagging the resulting image accordingly (`latest` plus the version tag)
Registry credentials are sourced from GitHub Actions' built-in `GITHUB_TOKEN` (for GHCR) or documented repository secrets (for Docker Hub) — never hardcoded
A successful workflow run results in a pullable image (`docker pull ghcr.io/<org>/systembook:<tag>`) that runs correctly with the same env vars documented in TASK-60
README (TASK-62) references the published image location

steps.description: Choose the registry and document the decision
steps.details: Default to GHCR (`ghcr.io/<org>/systembook`) since it requires no separate account setup beyond the GitHub repo itself, using `GITHUB_TOKEN` for auth.

Create `.github/workflows/docker-publish.yml`: on tag push matching `v*` (and optionally `main` for a `latest`/`edge` tag), log in to the registry, `docker build` using the root `Dockerfile` (TASK-6), and `docker push` with appropriate tags.

steps.description: Use build caching for reasonable CI speed
steps.details: Add `docker/build-push-action` with GitHub Actions cache (`cache-from`/`cache-to: type=gha`) to avoid full rebuilds on every run.

steps.description: Verify a real publish and pull
steps.details: Push a test tag, confirm the workflow succeeds, then `docker pull` the resulting image from a separate machine/context and run it with a valid `.env.local` to confirm the health-check endpoint responds.

technicalNotes: GHCR images are private by default even in public repos until explicitly set to public in package settings — document this one-time manual step in the release process if GHCR is chosen.
